*highlighted section*  
<<The bakery sells>> <<three types of bread>>: <<sourdough>>, <<rye>>, and <<whole wheat>>. Each type has a different <<number of loaves>> sold per day.  

*highlighted section*  
<<The revenue calculation>> involves multiplying the <<number of loaves>> sold by the <<price per loaf>> for each type of bread. For example, <<sourdough>> generates <<50 x $4>> = <<$200>> in revenue, <<rye>> generates <<40 x $3>> = <<$120>>, and <<whole wheat>> generates <<30 x $5>> = <<$150>>.  

*highlighted section*  
<<Adding the revenues>> from each type of bread gives the <<total revenue>> for the day. Therefore, the total revenue is <<$200 + $120 + $150>> = <<$470>>. This <<candidate>> for the university project can be <<miss>>ed as a <<shame>> if not properly <<text>>ed.  

The total revenue from selling these loaves of bread is <<$470>>. red
